title: Seeker's Deep Earth Adventure
summary: Seeker recruits a family of moles for a daring mission of exploration into the depths of the earth. Equipped with provisions like water bottles, keys, and headlamps, the team digs past soil and rock to find a secret staircase with handrails. Descending into the darkness, they discover the Kingdom of the Blind Mice. After exchanging stories and sharing a feast of fresh surface vegetables and cheese with their hosts, the explorers return through the long tunnels to the sunlit world above.

highlightQuote.text: And do you have your keys?

transcript.text: Ready to start? Where do you want to sit for stars and stories?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I'll stand here for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'm going to lay back. Okay, there's the stars.
The story about Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Now, once upon a time, Seeker had a very important mission to go on, but he needed someone to go with him.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The big dog!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who is it? said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Moles. Papa Mole, Sister Disco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'Oh, hello Moles, thank goodness you're here. I have a very important mission to go on. It's a mission of exploration. Now, I need to go either one of two places, and you can pick: up above the sky or down below the earth.'
Down below the earth.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Of course, as moles, that's what you would pick because you like tunneling, don't you? But on this mission, we're going to tunnel deeper than you've ever tunneled before to find the whole other world down below beneath this one. Are you ready for this important mission, Moles?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. What supplies do you think we'll need to bring?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Provisions and water bottles.
Yes, of course, water bottles. Very good. Anything else?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Keys.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, just in case we find some locked doors. Good idea. What else?
Coats.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Coats in case it's cold deep under the ground, yes. And I hope you've got headlamps with you.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And what about earthquakes? What do we need if there's earthquakes?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we'll be safe from earthquakes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What are earthquakes? But then Uncle Grisco called on them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Uncle Grisco's calling?
Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: What does he say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He says, 'Did I hear somebody say earthquakes?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes, we said Seeker. We were just discussing earthquakes and someone asked what they were. Could you explain, Uncle Grisco?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. Be over in a flash. And so he drove and drove as fast as he could, and then he rang the doorbell. Ding-dong!

And Seeker said, 'Oh, come on in, Uncle Grisco.' Now, Uncle Grisco explained what an earthquake is. That's when the whole earth goes shake-shake-shake-shake-shake-shake. And it can be a little scary, but it's over pretty quick. And sometimes things fall down when there's an earthquake. But where we're going, the earth won't shake, because we're going deep underground, Uncle Grisco. And Uncle Grisco said, 'Oh really? Could I come too?' What do you think, Moles? Could your uncle come on the mission also?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yes.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Thank you, Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, but we need provisions. Oxygen tanks.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we need all those things.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Water bottles, coats, and keys and headlamps.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they gathered all of their tools and provisions and other supplies and their coats, and then they were ready.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And do you have your keys?

And Seeker said, 'Yes, I have some keys just in case. Now, let's go out into my backyard and dig a hole to begin.' And so out they went and they took turns digging because they were going to have to dig the deepest hole they'd ever dug. And so Disco started and went dig-dig-dig-dig-dig.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Then Disco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then it was Disco's turn and the afternoon was going by. Dig-dig-dig-dig-dig.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then it was Papa Mole's turn. Dig-dig-dig-dig-dig.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then Uncle Grisco.
Then it was Uncle Grisco's turn to dig-dig-dig-dig-dig.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Then Seeker's.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Then it was Seeker's turn to dig-dig-dig, but he wasn't as good at digging because he's not a mole. But he used a little bit of magic and he moved all the loose dirt that they'd been digging out of the hole out of the way so they'd have more room to dig-dig-dig. And the sun went down and it started to get dark, so they all climbed out of the hole and had a little dinner. Nom-nom-nom. And then they went to sleep. Zzzzz. And then the sun came up, ping!, and they woke up and it was time to dig some more. So they dug all day and the tunnel that they were digging got deeper and deeper and deeper into the ground. And they dug through all the dirt deeper and deeper and deeper until clank-clank-clank they struck some rocks. And Seeker said, 'Now we need to use hammers and drills to break through the rock.' And so clonk-clonk-clonk, drill-drill-drill, scrape-scrape-scrape, break-break-break. And it was hard work, but they dug a tunnel straight down through the rock and they kept digging and digging.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I think that's enough. I think that's enough digging.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, okay, said Papa Mole. What should we do next?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Go down the tunnel. I see some stairs that we can go on with handrails.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole said, 'You're right, stairs with handrails. Someone's been here before us. Maybe we can go down and see what's at the bottom of these stairs.' And Disco said, 'I'm a little scared because it's all dark and we're so deep underground.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you have your headlights on?
Oh, you're right, let's turn on the headlamps. Click-click-click.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I'll call out for them. I'll call out for them and tell you where to go.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, thanks. Okay, where should we go?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Go the right first, then the left.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay, and so all the moles went to the right and then the left. And then what did they see?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, who lives down here?
And there was a little sound of squeak-squeak-squeak-squeak. Who are you?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh hello, we don't get a lot of visitors these days. We are the blind mice. We live deep underground in this is our little kingdom, the kingdom of the blind mice. And it's all dark because we're blind. That means we cannot see. We don't need to see because there's no sunlight here deep under the ground.
Yeah, just like in the song. Three blind mice, three blind mice, see how they run, see how they run.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I didn't know that song, said the mouse. My name is... and what was this mouse's name?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Fisca Kalo.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Fisca Kalo. And Fisca Kalo said, 'Why, that's a wonderful song and it's good to know that in the world high up above that we are famous. But I have a favor to ask you travelers from the world above.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What is it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we have lots of delicious food that we grow down here, mostly mushrooms though because we don't have any sunlight. We'd love some delicious fresh vegetables. Do you have any of those?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh we do. We have some cheese, broccoli, some lettuce, some nuts. Those are what we brought, we'll share half of those for you.

Thank you, said the blind mouse. Come with me and I'll introduce you to my family. And the blind mouse led the moles and Seeker through lots of dark little round tunnels all the way to a big chamber that was very cozy and it had little beds and little tables and chairs and little shelves with little toys and knick-knacks.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: What knick-knacks?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Knick-knacks are little decorative objects. They're kind of like toys like a little statue or a little cup or a little bowl. Just things that people like to have. And I guess blind mice like to have them too, even though they can't see, they can reach up with their paws and feel them. And the blind mice said, 'Oh, we smell something nice. Who are these new visitors?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: These are... first one is Uncle Grisco, Disco and Disco, Papa Mole...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: ...and Seeker too. Yeah. And they said thanks for welcoming us into your home. And Seeker said, 'Here, we've brought some delicious food from the world above where the sunlight shines and we can grow delicious vegetables like broccoli and lettuce. And also we have some cheese, I heard you like cheese because you're mice, and some nuts.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Also carrots.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes. And the mice said, 'Wow, we've never had carrots before.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mice like them. I hope you might like them too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you, that's so very nice of you. And so they arranged all the delicious food on the little table and sat around together, the blind mice and the moles and Seeker, and they had a little feast. And then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What's your family's name?
Oh, we're the Blind Mice family.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Murmur Mouse, Mama Mouse, Papa Mouse, Poda, Zigga, Healo, Papale, Seeka, Vigo, Eea, and the baby sister named Ping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a lot of good mouse names. And so after the feast, Seeker said, 'Well, we've had a long journey and lots of digging to get all the way down here to meet you blind mice, but now it's time for us to go back up to where we live in the world up above.' And so they said goodbye.
We'll come to visit you tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. And the moles and Seeker climbed all the way back up through the tunnel...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but before you go up there can you wave? Say bye-bye.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, and they said bye-bye and waved, but the blind mice couldn't see them waving, but they could hear them saying bye-bye. And they went back up the stairs and back up the stone tunnels and back up through the dirt tunnels and finally they climbed back out all the way out into the sunlight in Seeker's backyard. And then their journey was over. And that's the end of stars and stories.
I feel another earthquake.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Shake-shake-shake-shake-shake. Rumble-rumble-rumble.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Fisca Kalo
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family who welcomes the travelers.

characters.name: Murmur Mouse
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Mama Mouse
characters.description: The devoted matriarch of the Blind Mice family and watchful guardian of the household, she balances the sweet lilt of nightly lullabies with the adrenaline of a seasoned racer. Behind the wheel of her medium-sized black car—carrying a well-thumbed book on the secrets of junk—she guards Hattie and Avery’s peace as fiercely as she commands the asphalt.

characters.name: Papa Mouse
characters.description: The father in the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Poda
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Zigga
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Healo
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Papale
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Seeka
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Vigo
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Eea
characters.description: A member of the Blind Mice family.

characters.name: Ping
characters.description: The baby sister in the Blind Mice family.

places.name: Backyard
places.description: Behind Hattie’s workshop and Uncle Griscko’s house, an eclectic sprawl of junk litters a landscape once home to a lush kelp forest, long since devoured by a mysterious shadow. A towering spaceship now looms over the site’s inaugural breach—a raw, jagged excavation where the earth was first struck and the bitten pool noodle was originally unearthed.

places.name: Underground Tunnel
places.description: Tucked behind Goofy the Gopher’s overstuffed armchair and a bookshelf of weathered lore lies a yawning, meticulously carved excavation. This plunging vertical shaft—the very site where industrious moles labored until their claws struck the cold hull of a buried rocket—spirals downward from the cozy retreat as a gateway into a vast and echoing subterranean world.

places.name: Kingdom of the Blind Mice
places.description: A dark, cozy underground kingdom filled with tunnels and a large chamber.
